name: Olive Oil Greenway: Alcaudete Loop
name_original: Viaducto río Guadajoz por la Via Verde del Aceite - circular por los olivares de Alcaudete
description: Cycle a moderate 18.5-mile loop on the Via Verde del Aceite, featuring the Guadajoz River Viaduct and endless olive groves near Alcaudete.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alcaudete traverse a landscape characterized by extensive olive groves and rolling hills in the province of Jaén, Spain. The region features the Vía Verde del Aceite, a former railway line offering gentle gradients across diverse terrain. Beyond this greenway, the area includes the foothills of Sierra Ahillos and access to the Sierra Subbéticas Natural Park, providing varied elevations. Wetlands such as La Laguna Honda and La Laguna de Chinche add ecological interest, while the Guadajoz River carves through parts of the landscape. Best touring cycling routes around Alcaudete The most popular touring cycling route is Olive Oil Greenway: Alcaudete Loop, a 18.5 miles (29.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the Vía Verde del Aceite through olive groves. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Olive Oil Greenway – Jaén Cathedral loop from Castillo de la Encomienda del Víboras, a difficult 54.5 miles (87.7 km) path. This route extends along the Vía Verde, offering views of historical sites and the broader olive landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Vía Verde del Aceite – Alamedal Viaduct loop from Moeve, a 62.4 miles (100.4 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde del Aceite, often completed in about 6 hours 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcaudete is defined by vast olive groves, rolling hills, and the well-maintained Vía Verde del Aceite. The network offers options for various ability levels, from gentle greenway sections to more challenging routes in the surrounding hills. The routes in Alcaudete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcaudete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alcaudete?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Alcaudete, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with 2 easy, 9 moderate, and 20 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcaudete?
A: The pleasant Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling in Alcaudete. The weather is generally mild, perfect for enjoying the extensive olive groves and rolling hills without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling routes in Alcaudete?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Aceite is particularly suitable for families due to its gentle gradients (max. 2-3%) and well-maintained surface. It follows an old railway line, offering a safe and scenic experience through olive groves. While specific 'easy' routes are fewer, the Vía Verde sections are generally accessible.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Alcaudete?
A: Yes, Alcaudete offers several circular touring routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996369523/olive-oil-greenway-alcaudete-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olive Oil Greenway: Alcaudete Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 29.7 km circular trail that takes you through a beautiful section of the Vía Verde del Aceite.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Alcaudete?
A: Alcaudete's touring cycling routes are defined by vast olive groves, picturesque rolling hills, and sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite. You'll also encounter diverse terrain near the Sierra Ahillos and access to the Sierra Subbéticas Natural Park, offering more challenging elevations. Wetlands like La Laguna Honda and La Laguna de Chinche add ecological interest, providing habitats for various bird species.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy several interesting sights. Along the Vía Verde del Aceite, you'll cross impressive steel viaducts, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813616" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Víboras River Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. Other attractions include the imposing Calatravan Castle in Alcaudete, and natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4751189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Laguna del Conde&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant historical site nearby.

Q: Are there places to stop for food or rest along the Vía Verde del Aceite?
A: Yes, many of the former railway stations along the Vía Verde del Aceite have been converted into cafes or visitor centers, offering convenient stops for refreshments and rest. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4751177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Restaurant and Olive Oil Interpretation Center in the Old Luque Station&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcaudete?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. Touring cyclists often praise the well-maintained Vía Verde del Aceite, the stunning scenery of endless olive groves, and the variety of routes that cater to different abilities, from gentle greenway sections to more challenging hill climbs.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Alcaudete offers 20 difficult touring cycling routes for experienced riders. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadajoz River Viaduct – Zuheros Castle and Village loop from Alcaudete&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 148.5 km with significant elevation changes, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for cycling routes in Alcaudete?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, the Vía Verde del Aceite, being a popular greenway, typically has designated parking facilities at various access points and converted railway stations. Alcaudete itself, as a key point along the greenway, also offers parking options within the town.

Q: Are there any routes that connect Alcaudete to other towns or cities?
A: Yes, Alcaudete serves as a key point along the Vía Verde del Aceite, with routes connecting to other towns. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38252215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olive Oil Greenway – Jaén Cathedral loop from Castillo de la Encomienda del Víboras&lt;/a&gt; extends along the Vía Verde, offering a longer journey towards Jaén and its historical sites.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available from Alcaudete?
A: One of the longest and most challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadajoz River Viaduct – Zuheros Castle and Village loop from Alcaudete&lt;/a&gt;, spanning approximately 148.5 km. This difficult route offers an extensive exploration of the region's diverse landscapes and cultural heritage.
tips: Here you can stop to enjoy the views over this important and protected wetland. It is a temporary lagoon, so depending on the rains, it can be more or less full of water, dry or even full of flowers.

This colossal structure was made by French engineers back in the 1880s or 1890s in France, as it is formed from seamless rivets, very similar to the famous Eiffel Tower.
In general, all the viaducts of the Greenway of the Oil were built in France in the city of Creil by Daydé and Pillé.

It was a railway station that existed in the Spanish municipality of Alcaudete, in the province of Jaén, belonging to the defunct Linares-Puente Genil line. Currently, the old facilities have been rehabilitated and integrated into the space of the Aceite greenway.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 29.7 kilometers (18.5 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours and 15 minutes to complete on a touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this section of the Vía Verde del Aceite is generally considered easy to moderate, making it suitable for beginners and recreational cyclists. The route follows a former railway line, so gradients are gentle, typically not exceeding 2-3%.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in or near the town of Alcaudete, which provides good access to the Vía Verde del Aceite. Many old railway stations along the route have been converted and offer amenities, often including parking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of well-maintained surfaces, including paved sections and hard-packed gravel, making it ideal for touring bicycles. Approximately 75% of the route is dedicated cycleway, with some smaller portions on roads or other paths.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through vast olive groves, often referred to as Andalusia's 'sea of olives,' with some trees over a century old. The route also offers views of charming white villages and natural parks. Birdwatchers might spot flamingos and vultures, and in winter, almond blossoms add color, while spring brings wildflowers.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the impressive Guadajoz River Viaduct, which is a significant architectural marvel. You'll also enjoy Views over the Laguna del Conde and pass by the historic Alcaudete Station, which is a converted railway building.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the larger Vía Verde del Aceite and intersects with other official trails. You'll find sections that are also part of the Camino Mozárabe, Camino Natural Vía Verde del Aceite: Jaén a rio Guadajoz, and other segments of the Vía Verde del Aceite.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde del Aceite is generally accessible, specific dog policies can vary. It's usually permitted to bring dogs on a leash, especially on the dedicated cycle paths. However, always ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain, and carry water for them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle this section of the Vía Verde del Aceite. It is a public greenway accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes, restaurants, or bike rentals along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, several old railway stations along the Vía Verde del Aceite have been converted into cafes, restaurants, visitor centers, or even accommodations. These provide convenient stops for refreshments and services. Bike rental services are also available at various points, such as Doña Mencía's old station.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are generally the best times to cycle this route, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery with wildflowers in spring or comfortable conditions in autumn. Winter can also be nice, especially for almond blossoms, but summers can be very hot.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: Consider starting your ride early in the morning, especially during warmer months, to enjoy cooler temperatures. Take advantage of the converted railway stations along the route for breaks and to experience the local culture. Don't forget to look out for the numerous historic viaducts and tunnels that add to the route's unique character.

waypoints.name: Views over the Laguna del Conde
